explanation: Contradictory statements detected from the same speaker across turns
conversation_text: Hey B, I was reading this article about kids these days playing video games all night instead of helping with chores or learning real skills. Don't you think that's messing up society? Back in our grandparents' time, things were different. Yeah, A, totally. My grandpa always said, 'Idle hands make the devil's work.' Kids need to be out working the land or learning a trade, like our ancestors did. No screens, just real life. Exactly! Tradition says family comes first. Remember what our elders taught? 'The village raises the child,' but now everyone's glued to phones. Uh, it's like we're forgetting how to live right. Spot on. Ancestral wisdom from the old proverbs: 'As the twig is bent, so grows the tree.' If we don't bend 'em right with chores and respect, they're gonna grow crooked. My dad drilled that into us. Haha, yeah, my mom would quote that all the time. And think about marriage too – kids jumping into relationships without waiting, cohabiting and all. Our traditions say wait for the elders' blessing and a proper ceremony. That's why divorces were rare back then.
